# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder_14b)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { NextResponse } from 'next/server';
import type { NextRequest } from 'next/server';

// Define a function to validate the token (this is a placeholder)
function isValidToken(token: string): boolean {
  // Implement your token validation logic here
  return token === 'valid-token';
}

export async function middleware(req: NextRequest) {
  const authCookie = req.cookies.get('auth-token');

  if (!authCookie || !isValidToken(authCookie.value)) {
    // If no valid token, redirect to login or return a 401 Unauthorized response
    return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login', req.url));
  }

  // If authenticated, proceed with the request
  const res = NextResponse.next();

  // Optionally, you can add custom headers or modify the response here

  return res;
}

export const config = {
  matcher: ['/protected-route/:path*'],
};
